reasoning: The women's 100m breaststroke long course world record has been held by Lilly King since July 2017. As the cutoff date is October 26, 2025, we are entering the Northern Hemisphere winter season, which is traditionally dedicated to short course (25m) swimming (including the FINA World Cups and Short Course Championships). While some long course meets occur (such as the US Open in December), swimmers rarely peak for these meets, meaning the chance of a historic world record being broken during this period is extremely low. Given the record has stood for over 8 years and survived the Summer 2025 championship window, it is highly likely it will survive through January 2026. The probabilities decrease slightly to account for the longer time horizon passing the December meets (like the US Open) and early January prep meets, incorporating uncertainty as requested.
